verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family, which comprises sperm-egg fusion proteins. However, IZUMO4 itself is annotated as nuclear-resident by the NCBI summary with no documented plasma membrane localization, surface-trafficking cycling, or cell-state-induced externalization. The IZUMO family (IZUMO1, IZUMO2/SPARM1, IZUMO3) are characterized by single-pass TM anchoring and role in gamete fusion, but IZUMO4 diverges: it lacks the canonical TM topology and is reported as a nuclear protein. No evidence of documented cancer-cell ecto-display, cell-death PM exposure, activation-induced surface display, dual nuclear-PM localization, or wash-resistant TM-partner tethering. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs targeting IZUMO4 identified. The nuclear compartment assignment stands unchallenged.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO4 membership in the IZUMO family (canonically TM-anchored sperm-fusion proteins) creates a cross-family annotation conflict; however, the explicit nuclear localization annotation and absence of any documented PM pool or trafficking machinery sufficient to overcome the baseline nuclear assignment. Germline-restricted expression is expected for an IZUMO family member but does not relocate a nuclear protein to the outer leaflet.
